sections.title: 2022 Amendment
sections.text:
The following amendments were made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the 
forty-fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.

level=1
The following amendments were made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the

The following amendments were made to the text of the standard following decisions taken at the 
forty-fifth session of the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2022.

sections.title: STANDARD FOR CUMIN
sections.text:
CXS 327-2017  
Adopted in 2017. Amended in 2021, 2022.
sections.retrieval_text:
STANDARD FOR CUMIN
level=1
CXS 327-2017  
Adopted in 2017. Amended in 2021, 2022.

page_spans.text:
CXS 327-2017  
Adopted in 2017. Amended in 2021, 2022.

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to cumin offered for direct consumption, as an ingredient in food processing, or for repackaging if required. It excludes cumin intended for industrial processing.
sections.retrieval_text:
SCOPE
number=1
level=1
This standard applies to cumin offered for direct consumption, as an ingredient in food processing, or for repackaging if required. It excludes cumin intended for industrial processing.

page_spans.text: This standard applies to cumin offered for direct consumption, as an ingredient in food processing, or for repackaging if required. It excludes cumin intended for industrial processing.

sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS
number=3
level=1

sections.title: Product definition
sections.text: Cumin is the product prepared from “seeds”i of Cuminum cyminum L. of the Apiaceae family having reached appropriate degree of development for processing; and processed in an appropriate manner, undergoing operations such as cleaning, drying, grinding, and sifting.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION > Product definition
number=2.1
level=2
Cumin is the product prepared from “seeds”i of Cuminum cyminum L. of the Apiaceae family having reached appropriate degree of development for processing; and processed in an appropriate manner, undergoing operations such as cleaning, drying, grinding, and sifting.

page_spans.text: Cumin is the product prepared from “seeds”i of Cuminum cyminum L. of the Apiaceae family having reached appropriate degree of development for processing; and processed in an appropriate manner, undergoing operations such as cleaning, drying, grinding, and sifting.

sections.title: Styles
sections.text: Cumin may be offered in one of the following styles: a) whole/intact; b) cracked: broken into two or more pieces; and c) ground: processed into powders.
sections.retrieval_text:
DESCRIPTION > Styles
number=2.2
level=2

sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS > Quality factors
number=3.2
level=2

sections.title: Moisture content
sections.text: Cumin (whole, cracked or ground) must not contain more than 10 percent moisture.
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS > Quality factors > Moisture content
number=3.2.1
level=3
Cumin (whole, cracked or ground) must not contain more than 10 percent moisture.

page_spans.text: Cumin (whole, cracked or ground) must not contain more than 10 percent moisture.

sections.title: Odour, flavour and colour
sections.text: Cumin shall have a characteristic aroma and flavour which can vary depending on geo -climatic factors/conditions. Cumin shall be free from any foreign odour or flavour and especially from mustiness. Cumin shall have a characteristic colour varying from light grey to dark brown.
sections.retrieval_text:
ESSENTIAL COMPOSITION AND QUALITY FACTORS > Quality factors > Odour, flavour and colour
number=3.2.2
level=3
Cumin shall have a characteristic aroma and flavour which can vary depending on geo -climatic factors/conditions. Cumin shall be free from any foreign odour or flavour and especially from mustiness. Cumin shall have a characteristic colour varying from light grey to dark brown.

page_spans.text: Cumin shall have a characteristic aroma and flavour which can vary depending on geo -climatic factors/conditions. Cumin shall be free from any foreign odour or flavour and especially from mustiness. Cumin shall have a characteristic colour varying from light grey to dark brown.

sections.title: Classification
sections.text: Whole cumin may be classified in three classes/grades according to physical and chemical requirements as specified in Table 1 and Table 2. When ungraded, the provisions for class/grade III requirements apply as the minimum requirements.
sections.retrieval_text:

level=3

page_spans.text: Whole cumin may be classified in three classes/grades according to physical and chemical requirements as specified in Table 1 and Table 2. When ungraded, the provisions for class/grade III requirements apply as the minimum requirements.

sections.title: Chemical characteristics
sections.text: Whole, cracked and ground cumin shall comply with the chemical requirements specified in Table 2.

level=1
Whole, cracked and ground cumin shall comply with the chemical requirements specified in Table 2.

page_spans.text: Whole, cracked and ground cumin shall comply with the chemical requirements specified in Table 2.

sections.title: Classification of defectives
sections.text: A lot sample that fails to meet one or more of the applicable quality requirements, as set out in Section 3.2 (except those based on sample averages), should be considered as a defective.

sections.title: Lot acceptance
sections.text: A lot should be considered as meeting the applicable quality requirements referred to in Section 3.2 when the number of defectives, as defined in Section 3.3, does not exceed the acceptance number of the appropriate sampling plan. For factors evaluated on a sample average, a lot will be considered acceptable if the average meets the specified tolerance, and no individual sample is excessively out of tolerance.
sections.retrieval_text:

number=3.4
level=1

For factors evaluated on a sample average, a lot will be considered acceptable if the average meets the specified tolerance, and no individual sample is excessively out of tolerance.

page_spans.text: A lot should be considered as meeting the applicable quality requirements referred to in Section 3.2 when the number of defectives, as defined in Section 3.3, does not exceed the acceptance number of the appropriate sampling plan. For factors evaluated on a sample average, a lot will be considered acceptable if the average meets the specified tolerance, and no individual sample is excessively out of tolerance.

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Anticaking agents as listed in Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995)1 may be permitted for use in ground cumin only.

sections.text: The products covered by the provisions of this standard shall be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985).5 In addition, the following specific provisions apply:

sections.title: METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING
sections.text: For checking the compliance with this standard, the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999)7 relevant to the provisions in this standard, shall be used.

sections.text: It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Moisture Foods (CXC 75-2015)3 (Annex III) and other relevant Codex Alimentarius texts such as codes of hygienic practice and codes of practice.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR CUMIN
pages.summary: Página com título principal do documento e duas seções principais sem numeração, contendo textos explicativos sobre o padrão e emendas de 2022.

A página contém três seções principais, com a seção 2 subdividida em três subseções, e a seção 3 subdividida com várias subseções, algumas das quais continuam para a próxima página. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado e textos explicativos foram associados aos títulos corretamente para uso em RAG.

Página contém continuação da seção 3.2.5 Chemical characteristics com tabela e texto associado, e início da seção 3.3 Classification of defectives. Cabeçalho identificado, continuidade indicada para 3.2.5. Texto limpo para uso em RAG.

Página mapeada com 10 seções e subseções principais, mantendo a hierarquia dos níveis 1, 2 e 3 conforme numeração; destaque para continuidade correta e textos associados a cada nó, sem inclusão de cabeçalhos ou rodapés.

Mapa da página apresenta um único nó de nível 1: 'NOTES', contendo a lista sequencial de notas bibliográficas. O título 'NOTES' aparece claramente, configurando nível 1. O texto inclui as referências completas. Indica-se continuidade desde a página anterior, pois o conteúdo parece sessão contínua. Sem divisão adicional em subseções ou níveis inferiores. Cabeçalho repetitivo identificado e não incluído no texto interno.
